title: Panamax Freight View on Indonesian Thermal Coal Flows
section_path: Panamax Freight View on Indonesian Thermal Coal Flows
section_path_text: Panamax Freight View on Indonesian Thermal Coal Flows
summary:
Main
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed_caa894a8b64d.png
Key Takeaways
● Indonesian thermal coal flows to India (Panamax) softened toward late February alongside lower HBA pricing, while South African volumes strengthened YoY, indicating firmer positioning. ● The BDI held above 2,100, supported by Panamax, Supramax, and Handysize strength, while Capesize showed early signs of correction in the Atlantic and firmer Pacific sentiment. ● Ballaster counts point to rising Atlantic supply pressure and continued elevated vessel availability in Pacific Supramax and Handysize.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed_caa894a8b64d.png
Key Takeaways
● Indonesian thermal coal flows to India (Panamax) softened toward late February alongside lower HBA pricing, while South African volumes strengthened YoY, indicating firmer positioning. ● The BDI held above 2,100, supported by Panamax, Supramax, and Handysize strength, while Capesize showed early signs of correction in the Atlantic and firmer Pacific sentiment.

children.title: Chart of the Week
children.summary:
Panamax Freight View - Indonesia Thermal Coal to India
↓32% Month-on-Month - ↓40% Year-on-Year
This week's focus highlights the softening trend in Signal Ocean Platform data flows for Indonesian thermal coal shipments to India, particularly within the Panamax vessel segment, toward the end of February. The deceleration in shipments coincided with adjustments to Indonesia's reference coal pricing.

children.title: Freight Market Overview
children.summary: The Baltic Dry Index (BDI) has maintained momentum from the previous week, stabilizing above the 2,100-point threshold despite a slight daily dip. This level represents a significant year-on-year increase of +100%.

children.title: Capesize | Weaker
children.summary:
C3 Tubarao-Qingdao / C17 Saldanha Bay-Qingdao
Image reference: unnamed (3).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28329_1be7045bcda2.png
The rate for the Tubarao to Qingdao route held a similar weakening sentiment to the previous week, with rates around $24/ton (+28% YoY). Similarly, the Saldanha Bay-Qingdao rates continued to be assessed around $17/mt (+36% YoY).

children.title: Panamax | Firmer
children.summary:
P7 USG-Qingdao grain ($/mt) / P8 Santos-Qingdao ($/mt)
Image reference: unnamed (4).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28429_0b3719f33bab.png
Rates for the USG-Qingdao and Santos-Qingdao routes continue to hold firm. The Santos-Qingdao rate was assessed mid-week above $40/ton, marking a 22% increase year-over-year.

children.title: Supramax | Weaker
children.summary:
S4A US Gulf trip to Skaw-Passero
Image reference: unnamed (5).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28529_10d1e45b9c18.png
Rates on the USG-to-Skaw-Passero route saw a notable drop to $26k/d, a significant revision from last week's levels, which were above $30k/day. Despite this recent decline, current rates remain 76% higher than those recorded during the same period last year.

children.title: Handysize | Firmer
children.summary:
HS4_38 US Gulf trip via USG or NCSA to Skaw-Passero
Image reference: unnamed (6).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28629_6ca853a8675b.png
USG trip to Skaw-Passero recorded levels of around $24k/d, an increase of around $15k/d from mid-January and up by 128% YoY.

children.title: Capesize C5 | Firmer
children.summary:
C5 West Australia-Qingdao
Image reference: unnamed (7).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28729_e8486611fcd3.png
Rates for the West Australia-Qingdao route remained firm, hovering around $10/ton (+38% YoY). The recent low was observed in mid-January at approximately $7.5/ton.

children.title: Panamax | Firmer
children.summary:
P3A_82 HK-S Korea incl Taiwan, one Pacific RV / P5_82 South China, one Indonesian RV
Image reference: unnamed (8).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28829_b8131b7eee10.png
The Panamax Pacific market has maintained the substantial gains from the previous weeks of February. Specifically, rates on the P3A_82 route have climbed above $19k/d, marking about a $7k/day increase compared to the same period last year.

children.title: Supramax | Firmer
children.summary:
S2 North China one Australian/Pacific RV / S10 South China trip via Indonesia
Image reference: unnamed (9).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed28929_9b81fad1035b.png
The upward momentum in the Supramax Pacific market continued from the previous week. Notably, the S2 route experienced a spike, reaching approximately $16k/d, a significant monthly increase of 28%.

children.title: Handysize | Firmer
children.summary:
HS5_38 SE Asia to Singapore-Japan / HS6_38 N China-SK-Japan RV / HS7_38 to SE Asia
Image reference: unnamed (10).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281029_13bb47255f92.png
The Pacific Handysize freight market has shown early signs of recovery from its prior weaker trend. Rates for the HS5_38 and HS6_38 have firmed up to approximately $10k/d each, with the HS7_38 rate close behind at nearly $9.7k/d.

children.title: Capesize | 5D MA Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (11).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281129_baad88a7f973.png
Vessel supply notably increased in the Indian Ocean/South Africa area, where the count rose to 200, up from roughly 190 in the middle of last week's dry market monitor period. Similarly, the Far East/NOPAC region maintained a high supply level, registering approximately 130 vessels.

children.title: Panamax | 5D MA Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (12).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281229_acd49a12bfc1.png
The Indian Ocean maintained the spike from the previous week, holding above 300. In Australasia, the trend of decreasing ballasters continued, with the vessel count dropping by nearly 24 vessels to approximately 156.

children.title: Supramax | 5D MA Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (13).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281329_a4b9f37a2f37.png
The Pacific region continued to exhibit elevated vessel availability, a trend persisting from the preceding two weeks. The vessel count in the Far East/NOPAC has climbed notably, now standing at 196 (+17% WoW).

children.title: Handysize | 5D MA Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (14).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281429_3ad4147199b0.png
Handysize vessels continued to face elevated supply pressure. In the North Atlantic, the vessel count remained high, exceeding 200 (+13% WoW).

children.title: Capesize ↑ 5.2% WoW | Panamax ↑ 2.2% WoW
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (15).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281529_f1e7902a5d5b.png
The tonne-mile growth rate, measured on a Base 100 Index, rebounded for the larger vessel classes this week. The Capesize index climbed to 94.5, a notable increase of 4.7 points from the previous week's 89.8 (19 February 2026).

children.title: Supramax ↓ 1.8% WoW | Handymax ↑ 5.3% WoW | Handysize ↓ 4.6% WoW
section_path: Supramax ↓ 1.8% WoW | Handymax ↑ 5.3% WoW | Handysize ↓ 4.6% WoW
children.section_path_text: Supramax ↓ 1.8% WoW | Handymax ↑ 5.3% WoW | Handysize ↓ 4.6% WoW
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (16).png | assets/2026-02-27_panamax-freight-view-on-indonesian-thermal-coal-flows_img_unnamed281629_305164623d5e.png
The tonne-mile growth rate, measured on a Base 100 Index basis, showed mixed performance across the smaller vessel classes this week. The Handymax index climbed to 98.8, marking a strong increase of 4.9 points from 93.9 on 19 February 2026.
